question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a malicious record file whose consensusStart is less than or equal to the previous file's consensusEnd, triggering the silent skip path in shouldParse() so that onEnd() is never called and all transactions in that file are permanently dropped from the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted RecordFile with a null or empty previousHash field to bypass the hash chain check in shouldParse(), causing onEnd() to be invoked with an unverified file and allowing forged transactions to be persisted while legitimate ones are suppressed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a HashMismatchException to be thrown during shouldParse() by uploading a record file with a mismatched previousHash, halting the entire parse pipeline and preventing onEnd() from ever being called, thereby permanently suppressing all transactions in that file and all subsequent files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CompositeRecordStreamFileListener's sequential iteration over listeners so that if an early listener's onEnd() throws an ImporterException, all subsequent listeners never receive the onEnd() call, causing their transaction data to be silently dropped?
